citation.quote: Mining activities are projected to end in 2052 when the current reserves are expected to be exhausted. (effective date December 31, 2021); LOM copper 27.9 billion pounds
citation.section: derived: reserve / mine life; no single-year figure stated. 27.9 Blb x 0.4536 = 12,655 kt; 12,655 kt / 31 yr = 408 kt/yr. A near-term projected average of 0.99 Blb/yr (449 kt) for 2022-2026 appears in Table 19.3 but is a 5-yr period average.

reserves_kt.unit: kt contained Cu (recoverable basis; see caveat)

citation.quote: Life of Mine Plan ... Copper (billion pounds) 27.9
citation.section: Table 19.1 Economic Analysis (LOM Plan). 27.9 Blb x 0.4536 = 12,655 kt. CAVEAT: the Mineral Reserve Statement table cells were caption-only in the source HTML; this is LOM commercially-recoverable copper (recovery 88.4% applied), not strictly contained reserve.

citation.quote: Mining activities are projected to end in 2052 when the current reserves are expected to be exhausted.
citation.section: Section 13.2 Mine Plan Development; remaining life = end-year 2052 minus effective date 2021
